Suzuki V6 Escudo Pikes Peak SP '98 - 4WD - 800PP - Racing Medium Tires - All Track - SWAP - 170
Jun 19, 2026
800PP4WD
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** SWAP ENGINE: VR38DETT-GT-R-NISMO Rims 17" - Rim Width Wide - Offset Wide This is my fastest 800PP car at Spa, capable of a 2:00 lap in my hands. For the Sardegna event, use Fuel Map 5. It can run 3 laps per fuel stint and 6 laps on the tires, which is enough to win comfortably with about a 1-minute gap over second place. As for the driving characteristics, the car has huge power, outstanding grip, and a very precise front end. It also has plenty of rotation, so if you brake too late, you'll induce oversteer and a controllable drift. Try to avoid this, though, as it will cost you time. You also need to use the right amount of steering input to avoid upsetting the car's balance and driving line. I absolutely love this car for the Sardegna event. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Moderate performance at Sardegna, lapping around 1:34 to 1:35 with a one-stop strategy—using Fuel Map 1 and switching to Fuel Map 2 during the 8-lap stint. The car offers excellent grip and delivers a superb driving experience. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°

600PP4WD5 STARS
5 STARS CAR SETUP ***** Engine SWAP : R5-20vT-Quattro-Pikes Livery design : Atomarpulver Wide Car Rims "19 - Offset Wide - Rims wide Front A - Side A - Rear A - Wing A One of the fastest 600PP cars on the Nordschleife, it also performs exceptionally well on any technical track. Just adjust the gearbox with a shorter final drive to suit the circuit you're racing on. The car offers superior grip, good top speed, and excellent handling. Steering precision is outstanding, making it easy to place the car exactly where you want it. The only situation that requires extra attention is heavy braking during a sequence of corners, such as at the end of the last straight on the Nordschleife. I used a lot of rake and softer front springs to improve grip and responsiveness on corner entry and exit. Just avoid braking too late in these situations, and the car will remain stable and predictable. Lapping 6:57 in my hands Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°° https://youtu.be/ygFwzh8E5R8

Peugeot 9x8 '25 Gr1 - MR - 900PP - Racing Medium Tires - All Track - 1.70
Jun 14, 2026
900PPGr14WD
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** This is the fastest of the four latest Gr.1 cars in terms of top speed. It also has the most front-heavy weight distribution of the group, at 49/51. I'm not sure how much influence it has, but the Peugeot's front end feels a bit lazy off the brakes and through the apex. Most of its power is delivered high in the RPM range, and combined with the 49/51 weight distribution, the lighter rear end makes the car harder to control when exiting slow corners due to wheel spin. Because of this, the handling is still good—but not quite as good as the other three cars. That's why it doesn't earn a 5-star rating in my book. Enjoy your drive ><((((((°>°°°°°°

Toyota RAV4 Adventure '20 - 4WD - 600PP - Sport Medium Tires - Tokyo - SWAP - 1.70
Jun 13, 2026
600PP4WD5 STARS
5 STARS CAR SETUP ***** Swap Engine : Demon-Challenger Normal Body Rims "21 - Offset Wide - Rims wide -Front A - Side St - Rear B - Wing A OBS: Make sure to build the car using these exact engine and body parts. The setup has been developed around this specific configuration, and changing components may significantly affect the car's performance and balance. Crazy engine swap! There's a major PP bug on this car that allows it to carry 1,100 HP for the Tokyo rain event. Fuel consumption is high, requiring a two-stop strategy every four laps, but the performance is outstanding. I can lap in the 1:52 range, and the car offers superior grip with excellent driving characteristics. It's a real pleasure to drive, which is an important factor for players looking for a car to grind credits in GT7. Some cars may be fast, but they're also boring to drive. That's definitely not the case here. This car is both fast and genuinely enjoyable. 😉👌👍 Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

BMW M Hybrid V8 '25 Gr1 - MR - 900PP - Racing Medium Tires - All Track - 1.70
Jun 12, 2026
900PPGr1MR5 STARS
5 STARS CAR SETUP ***** All three of the latest Gr.1 cars have the same weight and power when tuned to 900PP. The main differences are their torque output and, in the Ferrari’s case, a slightly different front-to-rear weight distribution, likely due to its 4WD system. (I suppose) The BMW has less torque than the other two. To compensate at 900PP, you can run a more aggressive downforce balance to gain a bit more grip and cornering performance. Overall, the three cars are remarkably close in performance, whether on high-speed circuits or grip-oriented tracks. An amazing car. I'm not able to choose one over the others—I love all three of them. Enjoy your drive ><((((((°>°°°°°°

800PPFR5 STARS
5 STARS CAR SETUP ***** Engine SWAP : 8.0-WR16-64v4T-Chiron (Bugatti Chiron) -Size "20 & "21 - Rims Width wide - Offset wide -Front St - Rear A - Wing Custom Close to the performance of the very best 800PP cars, this machine earns a well-deserved 5-star rating thanks to its strong performance and excellent handling. I had to add a significant amount of rear ballast to manage the massive wheel spin caused by the high power output, then carefully rebalance the car to restore its handling characteristics—and the result was worth it. The car now performs exceptionally well at Spa and the Nordschleife, but it truly shines on Daytona Road Course, where it laps in the 1:32 range. Enjoy your drive. ><((((((°>°°°°°°°
